action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 2 achieved exploration_score=0.6013 (87.3% success, 344.9m avg distance). This is a huge improvement over the greedy local heuristic, but rooms are still problematic (74% success, 389m). Analysis: The global graph+Dijkstra approach works well, but replanning every step causes zigzagging and backtracking, inflating travel distance and wasting steps. Some episodes also barely fail (explored_rate ~0.9998) because the robot stops when no node has utility>0 but small unexplored pockets remain. Decision: Implement target commitment (stick to a chosen target for up to 8 steps or until reached/invalid) to reduce path inefficiency. Add stronger visited penalty, step-budget awareness, and a fallback to navigate toward the nearest frontier when no utility nodes exist. This should boost success rate (especially rooms) and reduce mean distance. Keep dummy checkpoint, run smoke test, submit eval.
action.command:
python train.py && python -c "
from cogniplan_env import ExplorationEnv
import glob, os
maps = glob.glob('/data/maps_train/room/*.png')
env = ExplorationEnv(map_path=maps[0])
from predict import run_episode
m = run_episode(env)
print('Smoke test metrics:', m)
"
